IND.identity: World's largest democracy and most populous nation. Rising economic and geopolitical power balancing Western alignment with strategic autonomy.
IND.current_position: Positioning as alternative manufacturing hub to China; deepening US/QUAD ties while retaining Russia relationship; navigating border tensions with China and Pakistan.
IND.history_brief: Ancient civilization with continuous cultural lineage. Mughal empire 16th–18th centuries, British colonial rule 1858–1947. Independence 1947 under Nehru, with Partition creating Pakistan. Socialist-planned economy until 1991 liberalization unleashed growth. Tech boom from 2000s. Rising global power from 2010s.

allies.reason: Defense cooperation, Rafale jets, strategic partner

tensions.reason: Border disputes, LAC tensions, strategic rivalry

CHN.identity: World's second-largest economy and primary peer competitor to the US. Civilization-state with Communist Party authority positioning itself as a global alternative to the liberal order.
CHN.current_position: Navigating US decoupling pressure; tightening party control under Xi; pursuing tech self-sufficiency in semiconductors; expanding Belt and Road and diplomatic influence in Global South.
CHN.history_brief: Millennia of dynastic rule. Qing collapse 1911, warlord era, civil war. PRC founded 1949 under Mao. Cultural Revolution 1966–76. Deng Xiaoping reforms from 1978 transformed economy. WTO accession 2001 accelerated growth. Xi Jinping consolidated power from 2012 onward.

allies.reason: No-limits partnership, energy trade, anti-US alignment

tensions.reason: South China Sea disputes, Scarborough Shoal

JPN.identity: Advanced island nation and technological powerhouse. Post-war pacifist constitution slowly loosening as China's rise changes the neighborhood. Core US ally in Asia.
JPN.current_position: Rearming in response to China and North Korea; deepening QUAD and US alliance; hosting more US military capacity; diversifying supply chains away from China.
JPN.history_brief: Feudal shogunate era, Meiji Restoration 1868 modernized rapidly. Imperial expansion led to WWII defeat. US occupation and pacifist constitution 1947. Economic miracle through 1980s. Lost decades of stagnation from 1990s. Abenomics revival attempt from 2012. Now shifting toward strategic activism.

working.reason: Growing defense ties, shared China concerns

tensions.reason: Senkaku islands, historical and strategic rivalry

KOR.identity: Advanced democracy that transformed from war-torn poverty to high-tech exporter in two generations. Semiconductor, automotive, and cultural (K-pop, cinema) powerhouse living under permanent North Korean shadow.
KOR.current_position: Tilting decisively toward US and Japan under recent administrations; squeezed between US semiconductor restrictions on China and Chinese market exposure; building nuclear latency debate.
KOR.history_brief: Japanese colonial rule 1910–45. Division and Korean War 1950–53. Military dictatorships through 1987. Democratization and economic takeoff. IMF crisis 1997, rapid recovery. Now a developed high-tech democracy with ongoing NK confrontation.

allies.reason: Trilateral cooperation with US, shared concerns

basic.name: North Korea
basic.government: Totalitarian one-party state under Kim dynasty

PRK.identity: The world's most isolated state. Nuclear-armed totalitarian dynasty under Kim Jong Un. Economy sustained by sanctions evasion and China/Russia backing.
PRK.current_position: Providing artillery and missiles to Russia for Ukraine war in exchange for tech and energy; accelerating nuclear and missile programs; rejecting any US dialogue on denuclearization.
PRK.history_brief: Soviet-installed Kim Il Sung regime post-1945. Korean War 1950–53. Kim dynasty succession: Kim Jong Il (1994), Kim Jong Un (2011). Nuclear tests from 2006. Brief Trump-Kim engagement 2018–19 collapsed. Deepening Russia alignment since 2022.

allies.reason: Arms for Ukraine war, expanding strategic ties

MNG.identity: Vast, sparsely populated landlocked democracy sandwiched between China and Russia. Pursues 'third neighbor' foreign policy courting the US, Japan, and EU to balance its giant neighbors.
weaknesses: Extreme dependence on China for exports
MNG.current_position: Benefiting from Western interest in non-China critical minerals; balancing pressure from Russia and China while deepening US and Japanese ties.
MNG.history_brief: Mongol Empire under Genghis Khan 13th century. Qing rule, independence 1911. Soviet satellite 1924–90. Peaceful democratic transition 1990. Mining boom 2010s followed by volatility. Actively cultivating third-neighbor partners.

TWN.identity: Self-governing democracy that China claims as its territory. Home to TSMC, the world's most important semiconductor manufacturer. A single flashpoint that could reshape the global economy and trigger great-power war.
TWN.current_position: Building asymmetric defense and societal resilience against Chinese coercion; deepening informal security ties with US, Japan, and Europe; TSMC globalizing fabs to US, Japan, Germany under pressure.
TWN.history_brief: Qing, then Japanese colony 1895–1945. ROC government retreated here after 1949 civil war defeat. Martial law until 1987. Democratization from 1990s. First non-KMT president 2000. Economic miracle centered on electronics then semiconductors. PRC pressure intensifying since 2016.

allies.reason: Taiwan Relations Act, primary security backer

working.reason: Strong informal ties, shared China concerns
One of few European countries with active engagement
Opened trade office under Taiwan name

tensions.reason: Sovereignty claim, military coercion, existential threat

PAK.identity: Nuclear-armed Muslim-majority state with a powerful military that shapes politics and foreign policy. Deep structural partner of China and rival of India. Chronically on the edge of economic crisis.
PAK.current_position: Navigating severe economic crisis with IMF support; deepening China dependence; tense relations with Afghan Taliban and rising TTP violence; India relations frozen.
PAK.history_brief: Created 1947 at Partition. Three wars with India. Bangladesh broke away 1971. Nuclear tests 1998. Alternating civilian and military rule (Musharraf 1999–2008). Post-2001 frontline in US War on Terror. Deepened China alignment via CPEC from 2015.

BGD.identity: Densely populated delta nation and the world's second-largest garment exporter. Underwent dramatic political rupture in 2024 with the ouster of Sheikh Hasina; now navigating transition under interim leadership.
BGD.current_position: Interim government under Muhammad Yunus managing post-Hasina transition; recalibrating relations with India (which backed Hasina) while maintaining ties with China; preparing for elections.
BGD.history_brief: East Pakistan until 1971 liberation war (Indian military support). Assassinations and military coups. Democracy restored 1990. BNP and Awami League alternated until Sheikh Hasina's extended rule 2009–2024. Student-led uprising toppled her August 2024. Now in transition.

working.reason: Complex: major trade partner, recently tense post-Hasina

LKA.identity: Island nation recovering from catastrophic 2022 economic collapse. Strategic location in Indian Ocean makes it contested ground between India and China.
LKA.current_position: Under IMF bailout program; restructuring sovereign debt including to Chinese creditors; new leftist-led government under Dissanayake balancing India and China; rebuilding tourism.
LKA.history_brief: Colonial rule (Portuguese, Dutch, British) until 1948 independence. 26-year Tamil civil war ended 2009. Rajapaksa family dominance. Chinese-financed Hambantota port leased out 2017 after debt troubles. Economic collapse 2022 forced president to flee. New political era under NPP government from 2024.

working.reason: Major creditor and investor, port infrastructure

NPL.identity: Himalayan republic wedged between India and China. Chronic political instability with frequent government changes. Remittances from migrant workers keep the economy afloat.
NPL.current_position: Balancing India (open border, cultural ties) and China (infrastructure, BRI member); hydropower export deals with India; periodic territorial frictions over Kalapani region.
NPL.history_brief: Unified in 18th century under Shah dynasty. Rana autocracy. Constitutional monarchy. Maoist insurgency 1996–2006 killed ~17,000. Monarchy abolished 2008, federal republic established 2015. Revolving-door coalitions since.

working.reason: Open border, dominant trade partner, cultural ties

BTN.identity: Small Himalayan kingdom known for Gross National Happiness philosophy. Deeply aligned with India for security and economy. No formal diplomatic ties with China despite shared border.
BTN.current_position: Quietly negotiating border demarcation with China; expanding Gelephu Mindfulness City project; maintaining close India security partnership amid Chinese pressure.
BTN.history_brief: Unified in 17th century. Treaty with British India 1910. Treaty with independent India 1949. Monarchy introduced democracy in 2008. Fourth king's GNH philosophy globally influential. Fifth king now on throne.

MDV.identity: Tiny Indian Ocean archipelago strategically contested by India and China. Luxury tourism drives the economy. Climate change is an existential threat to its low-lying islands.
MDV.current_position: Under Muizzu government, tilting toward China and away from India ('India Out' policy); reducing Indian military presence; deepening Chinese investment and infrastructure.
MDV.history_brief: Sultanate for centuries. British protectorate 1887–1965. Republic 1968. Gayoom authoritarian rule 1978–2008. Democratic transitions since with frequent reversals. Gradual China alignment accelerated under Muizzu from 2023.

working.reason: Growing preferred partner under current government
Historic partner, now strained but still major

snapshot.economy_type: Collapsed formal economy, aid and opium

AFG.identity: Landlocked South-Central Asian state under Taliban rule since August 2021. Unrecognized by any major power. Humanitarian crisis with severe restrictions on women's rights.
AFG.current_position: Taliban consolidating rule without international recognition; tensions with Pakistan over TTP; cautious engagement from China, Russia, and regional states; severe gender apartheid.
AFG.history_brief: Great Game buffer state. Soviet invasion 1979–89. Civil war, Taliban takeover 1996. US-led invasion 2001 after 9/11. 20-year republic collapsed August 2021 as US withdrew. Taliban back in power.

IRN.identity: Shia theocracy and regional power projecting influence through proxies across the Middle East. In direct confrontation with Israel, US, and Arab rivals. Near-threshold nuclear state.
IRN.current_position: Regional position severely weakened after Israeli campaigns against Hamas, Hezbollah, and direct strikes on Iran; Assad fall in Syria cut Hezbollah supply lines; nuclear program decisions looming; domestic protests recurring.
IRN.history_brief: Persian Empire. Constitutional Revolution 1906. Pahlavi dynasty, CIA-backed coup 1953 restored Shah. Islamic Revolution 1979 under Khomeini. Iran-Iraq War 1980–88. Nuclear deal 2015, US withdrawal 2018. Mahsa Amini protests 2022. Regional setbacks 2023–25.

allies.reason: Strategic partnership, drone supply, anti-US alignment
Historic ally, though weakened by Assad fall
